title: Stachys recta subsp. labiosa
what_description: Stachys recta subsp. labiosa is a plant species belonging to the family Lamiaceae. It is a perennial herb with a height of 30-100 cm, having erect stems and ovate, cordate leaves that are 4-12 cm long, with a cuneate base and a serrate margin. The leaves are dark green on the upper surface and pale green on the lower surface, with a short petiole. The inflorescence is a terminal spike with purple flowers, which are bilabiate and have a zygomorphic corolla, with the upper lip being 12-15 mm long and the lower lip being 10-12 mm long. The flowers are produced from June to September.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Stachys recta subsp. labiosa are inflammation, gastrointestinal issues, and digestive issues, which can be alleviated by its anti-inflammatory properties and ability to regulate bowel movements. Diarrhea and gastroenteritis are also effectively managed with this plant due to its antimicrobial and astringent properties. Fever reduction and wound healing are facilitated by its analgesic and antiseptic properties, while skin irritations such as eczema are soothed by its anti-inflammatory and moisturizing effects. Cough and respiratory problems are relieved by its expectorant properties, and indigestion is alleviated by its carminative properties. Additionally, the plant has been shown to be effective against fungal infections, colic, respiratory infections, vomiting, rheumatism, nausea, and anxiety.
properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Stachys recta subsp. labiosa are exhibited through its antimicrobial activity, which is primarily attributed to the presence of volatile oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids in this plant. The antioxidant properties of Stachys recta subsp. labiosa have been associated with its ability to scavenge free radicals and protect against oxidative stress, largely due to the bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The anti-inflammatory effects of this plant are mainly responsible for its potential in treating various inflammatory conditions, a property that is attributed to the presence of saponins and volatile oils. Additionally, Stachys recta subsp. labiosa has been found to possess antispasmodic properties, which are thought to be mediated by the flavonoids and phenolic acids present in the plant, thereby reducing muscle spasms and cramps. The analgesic properties of this plant are largely attributed to the bioactive compounds such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and volatile oils, which contribute to its pain-relieving effects.

constituents.name: stachys recta subsp. labiosa iridoid glycosides

constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Stachys recta subsp. labiosa are rosmarinic acid, quercetin, caffeic acid, apigenin, and luteolin, which are responsible for its medicinal properties due to their antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. Rosmarinic acid exhibits potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, scavenging free radicals and inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines. Quercetin also shows anti-inflammatory activity by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, while caffeic acid has antimicrobial properties, inhibiting the growth of bacteria and fungi. Apigenin and luteolin are flavonoids with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, exhibiting their activity by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and free radicals. These compounds work synergistically to provide the medicinal properties of Stachys recta subsp. labiosa, justifying its use in traditional medicine for treating various ailments.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Stachys recta subsp. labiosa are tea and infusion which are traditionally used as a mild expectorant to relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs due to its rich content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. Tincture is prepared by steeping the plant in a solvent like ethanol or glycerin to create a concentrated extract that can be used to treat respiratory problems and as an antispasmodic to soothe muscle cramps. Salve is prepared by infusing the plant in a carrier oil like olive or coconut oil to create an anti-inflammatory topical treatment for skin irritations and wounds. Infused oil is prepared by steeping the plant in a carrier oil to create a topical treatment for skin and wound care due to its antimicrobial properties. These preparations are beneficial due to the plant's content of essential oils like borneol and camphor that exhibit antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
side_effects_description: The possible side effects of using Stachys recta subsp. labiosa improperly include inducing nausea and causing vomiting, which can be due to the presence of toxic compounds such as sesquiterpene lactones and phenolic acids. These compounds can also exacerbate existing allergies, leading to severe reactions in individuals who are sensitive to them. Furthermore, Stachys recta subsp. labiosa contains volatile oils that may cause stomach upset, including nausea and vomiting, especially when ingested in large quantities. The improper use of this plant may also lead to gastrointestinal irritation, resulting in symptoms such as nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. In rare cases, the improper use of Stachys recta subsp. labiosa can cause severe allergic reactions, including anaphylaxis, which requires immediate medical attention.
